title: Ellu Urundai (Sesame Ladoo)

ingredients.name: white sesame seeds (or black, or a mix)

subs.name: a 1:1 mix of sesame and roasted peanuts for a nuttier, chunkier version — reduce sesame to 1 cup and add 1 cup peanuts
palm jaggery (karupatti) for a more complex, smoky flavour — the urundais will be slightly softer
a pinch of dry ginger powder (soonth) for a warmer, more pungent flavour

steps.text: Heat a wide, dry kadai over medium flame. Add the sesame seeds and roast, stirring continuously, for 5–7 minutes until they pop and crackle, turn a shade darker (white sesame becomes pale gold; black sesame becomes fragrant and slightly glistening) and smell nutty. Do not walk away — sesame can go from perfectly toasted to burnt in seconds. Transfer immediately to a wide plate and cool.

Combine the grated jaggery and water in a small heavy-bottomed saucepan or the same wiped-clean kadai over medium heat. Stir until the jaggery dissolves fully into a liquid. Switch to medium-high and cook without stirring for 4–5 minutes until it reaches soft-ball stage: drop a small spoonful into a cup of cold water — if it forms a soft, pliable ball that you can press between your fingers without it dissolving, it is ready. If it disappears in the water, cook a minute more; if it sets rock-hard, you have gone too far.

Remove the jaggery syrup from heat immediately. Add the roasted sesame seeds and cardamom all at once and stir vigorously to coat every seed in syrup. Work fast — the mixture cools quickly and becomes difficult to shape.

Lightly grease your palms with a few drops of neutral oil. Take a heaped tablespoon of the hot sesame-jaggery mixture and press it firmly in your palm, then roll between both palms into a tight round ball. The mixture should still be warm and slightly sticky. Shape all the urundais within 5 minutes while the mixture is workable; if it hardens, return the pan to low heat for 30 seconds.

Arrange the shaped urundais on a greased plate and leave to set and cool completely, about 20 minutes. They will firm up into a satisfying crunch-and-chew texture. Store in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 2 weeks.

substitution_notes: jaggery → palm jaggery (karupatti) for a more complex, smoky flavour — the urundais will be slightly softer; sesame-seeds → a 1:1 mix of sesame and roasted peanuts for a nuttier, chunkier version — reduce sesame to 1 cup and add 1 cup peanuts; cardamom → a pinch of dry ginger powder (soonth) for a warmer, more pungent flavour
